title: Q5® Site-Directed Mutagenesis
author: New England Biolabs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.besrjed6

steps.instruction: Assemble the following reagents in a thin-walled PCR tube. %s %s 25 μl RXN FINAL CONC. Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 12.5 μl 1X 10 μM Forward Primer 1.25 μl 0.5 μM 10 μM Reverse Primer 1.25 μl 0.5 μM Template DNA (1–25 ng/μl) 1 μl 1-25 ng Nuclease-free water 9.0 μl E0552 Q5 PCR Mixture by New England Biolabs

Transfer to a thermocycler and perform the following cycling conditions: Thermocycling Conditions for a Routine PCR: %s %s STEP TEMP TIME Initial Denaturation 98°C 30 seconds 25 Cycles 98°C 10 seconds 50–72°C* 10–30 seconds 72°C 20–30 seconds/kb Final Extension 72°C 2 minutes Hold 4–10°C
Assemble the following reagents: E0552 KLD Mixure by New England Biolabs
Mix well by pipetting up and down.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Thaw a tube of NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli cells on ice.
Add 5 μl of the KLD mix from the "KLD Section" above to the tube of thawed cells.
Carefully flick the tube 4-5 times to mix. Do not vortex.
Place the mixture on ice for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Pipette 950 μl of room temperature SOC into the mixture. undetermined SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B9020S
Incubate at 37°C for 60 minutes with shaking (250 rpm). 01:00:00
Mix the cells thoroughly by flicking the tube and inverting.
Spread 50-100 μl onto a selection plate.
